learning_outcomes.description: Evaluate global Human Resource Management challenges for inclusive, equitable and responsible global workforce participation.
Apply HRM practices and Indigenous principles to develop inclusive solutions to global HRM challenges.
Collaborate effectively to develop understandings of diverse stakeholders and communities to improve global HR.
Recommend ethical managerial actions to be taken to improve global HR practices.

assessments.task_description:
• Form: 5 closed-book quizzes to be completed individually.
  • Questions: 6 multiple choice questions per quiz.
  • Condition: All quizzes count towards your overall quiz mark.
  • Medium: Exclusively online (in class).
  • Timing:
  • Chapter 1 content covered in Week 1&2 will be used in Week 4 during a Trial Quiz.
  • Chapter 2 content covered in Week 3&4 will be assess in Week 5 (Quiz 1).
  • Chapter 5 content covered in Week 5&6 will be assessed in Week 7 (Quiz 2).
  • Chapter 6 content covered in Week 7 will be assessed in Week 8 (Quiz 3).
  • Chapter 7 content covered in Week 8 will be assessed in Week 9 (Quiz 4).
  • Chapter 8 content covered in Week 9&10 will be assessed in Week 10 (Quiz 5).

assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
25/09/2026 5:00 pm

Submit your written report via the Turnitin submission link located in the Assessments section of Blackboard.
assessments.task_description:
The Global Mobility Handbook is a team-based assessment comprised of a written report.

In this project, teams are required to analyse a global recruitment and selection challenge within an emerging market context. Drawing on relevant theory and evidence, students are expected to describe, evaluate, and critically analyse the challenge and develop practical recommendations for organisations operating internationally.

The written report should be approximately 2,000 words or a maximum of eight pages, whichever limit is reached first.

Following submission of the report, students will complete a peer evaluation of team member contributions. Peer evaluation outcomes may be used to adjust individual marks awarded for the project.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: Student teams will present a concise consulting pitch based on their Global Mobility Project. Acting as Global Mobility Consultants, teams will identify a key global recruitment and selection challenge, evaluate its implications within an emerging market context, and propose evidence-based recommendations for organisational practice. Through the pitch and subsequent discussion, students will demonstrate their ability to communicate complex global HR issues, justify managerial recommendations, and apply course concepts to realistic international business scenarios.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The team pitch will be delivered live in class and recorded for quality assurance purposes. No PowerPoint slides are required. All team members are expected to participate in the presentation and respond to follow-up questions from the teaching team.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: This assessment is designed to support your development as a culturally agile professional. You will complete a Cultural Agility self-assessment, undertake three cultural agility development activities, and conduct an interview with a person from a cultural background different from your own. You will then critically reflect on these experiences and consider how they have influenced your understanding of culture, diversity, and intercultural interactions. Evidence of activity completion and a transcript of the interview must be included as appendices. The journal should be no more than 1,500 words (±10%), excluding appendices.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submit your written report via the Turnitin submission link located in the Assessments section of Blackboard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Course Introduction

This week marks the introduction to the course, where we will outline the course structure, expectations, and key themes that will guide us throughout the semester. We will also set the stage for our Global HRM learning journey by exploring the concept of globalisation, covering what it is, the major forces that drive it, as well as why and how it matters in relation to Global HRM.


Learning outcomes: L01
learning_activities.topic_description: Course Introduction This week marks the introduction to the course, where we will outline the course structure, expectations, and key themes that will guide us throughout the semester. We will also set the stage for our Global HRM learning journey by exploring the concept of globalisation, covering what it is, the major forces that drive it, as well as why and how it matters in relation to Global HRM.

learning_activities.topic:
Introduction to Global HRM

This week, we will deepen our understanding of Global HRM by examining core concepts such as expatriate management, the distinctions between domestic and Global HRM, and the increasing complexity and challenges shaping the Global HRM landscape.

Learning outcomes: L01
learning_activities.topic_description: Introduction to Global HRM This week, we will deepen our understanding of Global HRM by examining core concepts such as expatriate management, the distinctions between domestic and Global HRM, and the increasing complexity and challenges shaping the Global HRM landscape.

learning_activities.topic:
Global HR and Diversity & Inclusion, Part 1

This week, we turn our attention to the powerful role of culture in shaping Global HRM practices. We will explore a range of cultural concepts, consider how culture impacts Global HRM, and begin developing the skills and empathy needed for cultural literacy. You will be introduced to tools that support cultural understanding and be encouraged to reflect on your own intercultural experiences and learning.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Global HR and Diversity & Inclusion, Part 1 This week, we turn our attention to the powerful role of culture in shaping Global HRM practices. We will explore a range of cultural concepts, consider how culture impacts Global HRM, and begin developing the skills and empathy needed for cultural literacy. You will be introduced to tools that support cultural understanding and be encouraged to reflect on your own intercultural experiences and learning.

learning_activities.topic:
Global HR and Diversity & Inclusion, Part 2

This week, we explore the concept of intersectionality and how overlapping identities such as race, gender, socio-economic status, and neurodivergence shape individual experiences in the global workplace. With a specific focus on neurodiversity, we will examine its impact on Global HR practices and how to foster inclusive, supportive environments for neurodivergent employees through intentional policies, leadership, and workplace design. You will complete a Trial Quiz which covers content delivered in Week 1&2.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Global HR and Diversity & Inclusion, Part 2 This week, we explore the concept of intersectionality and how overlapping identities such as race, gender, socio-economic status, and neurodivergence shape individual experiences in the global workplace. With a specific focus on neurodiversity, we will examine its impact on Global HR practices and how to foster inclusive, supportive environments for neurodivergent employees through intentional policies, leadership, and workplace design. You will complete a Trial Quiz which covers content delivered in Week 1&2.

learning_activities.topic:
Sourcing Global Talent, Part 1

This week, we explore various organisational staffing policies, the evolution of expatriate and inpatriate assignment management, and identify key factors that contribute to international assignment failure. You will also complete Quiz 1 which covers content delivered in Week 3&4.
learning_activities.topic_description: Sourcing Global Talent, Part 1 This week, we explore various organisational staffing policies, the evolution of expatriate and inpatriate assignment management, and identify key factors that contribute to international assignment failure. You will also complete Quiz 1 which covers content delivered in Week 3&4.

learning_activities.topic:
Sourcing Global Talent, Part 2

This week, you will engage in a case study designed to help you understand and work with the key factors involved in selecting an international assignee for an expatriate assignment.
learning_activities.topic_description: Sourcing Global Talent, Part 2 This week, you will engage in a case study designed to help you understand and work with the key factors involved in selecting an international assignee for an expatriate assignment.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Performance Management

This week, we examine multinational performance management at both global and local levels, analyse key factors influencing expatriate performance, explore performance management for expatriates and non-expatriates, including those on non-standard assignments, and examine challenges related to appraising international employees. You will also complete Quiz 2 which covers content delivered in Week 5&6.
learning_activities.topic_description: Global Performance Management This week, we examine multinational performance management at both global and local levels, analyse key factors influencing expatriate performance, explore performance management for expatriates and non-expatriates, including those on non-standard assignments, and examine challenges related to appraising international employees. You will also complete Quiz 2 which covers content delivered in Week 5&6.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Training and Development

This week, we explore the crucial role that training and development play in supporting the adjustment and retention of international assignees, while also exploring the key re-entry and career-related challenges international assignees may face. You will complete Quiz 3 which covers content delivered in Week 7.
learning_activities.topic_description: Global Training and Development This week, we explore the crucial role that training and development play in supporting the adjustment and retention of international assignees, while also exploring the key re-entry and career-related challenges international assignees may face. You will complete Quiz 3 which covers content delivered in Week 7.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Compensation, Part 1

This week, we examine the complexities of transitioning from domestic to international compensation, detail the key components of international compensation programs, and outline the two main approaches to international compensation along with their advantages and disadvantages. You will also complete Quiz 4 which covers content delivered in Week 8.
learning_activities.topic_description: Global Compensation, Part 1 This week, we examine the complexities of transitioning from domestic to international compensation, detail the key components of international compensation programs, and outline the two main approaches to international compensation along with their advantages and disadvantages. You will also complete Quiz 4 which covers content delivered in Week 8.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Compensation, Part 2

This week, we examine emerging approaches to international compensation, analyse key challenges related to taxation and benefits schemes, and evaluate the corporate and expatriate return on investment across different types of international assignments. You will also complete Quiz 5 which covers content delivered in Week 9&10.
learning_activities.topic_description: Global Compensation, Part 2 This week, we examine emerging approaches to international compensation, analyse key challenges related to taxation and benefits schemes, and evaluate the corporate and expatriate return on investment across different types of international assignments. You will also complete Quiz 5 which covers content delivered in Week 9&10.

learning_activities.topic:
Global Mobility Consulting Pitches

Across Weeks 11 and 12, student teams will present a concise consulting pitch based on their Global Mobility Project. Acting as Global Mobility Consultants, teams will identify a key global recruitment and selection challenge, evaluate its implications within an emerging market context, and propose evidence-based recommendations for organisational practice. Through the pitch and subsequent discussion, students will demonstrate their ability to communicate complex global HR issues, justify managerial recommendations, and apply course concepts to realistic international business scenarios.

learning_activities.topic:
Fireside Chat

This week, we welcome a special guest for an exclusive fireside chat. This interactive session will offer unique insights and real-world perspectives, giving you the opportunity to engage directly and deepen your understanding of Global HRM.
